description:
A DevSecOps policy requires that every Dockerfile in the repository must:

  1. Use the exact base image string “alpine:3.18”.
  2. Explicitly switch to an unprivileged user called “appuser”.

  The repository already contains one Dockerfile that is out of compliance:

    /home/user/app/Dockerfile

  Current, non-compliant contents of that file:
  
      FROM alpine:latest
      CMD ["echo", "Hello World"]

  Your assignment:

  1. Bring the Dockerfile into compliance with the two rules above.
     • Replace the existing FROM line with “FROM alpine:3.18”.
     • Append the directive “USER appuser” as the final instruction in the Dockerfile
       (after the existing CMD instruction).

  2. Produce a very small compliance-evidence log so that an automated test can verify
     your edits.  Create the file

        /home/user/policy/update_log.txt

     The log must contain exactly two lines (no blank lines, no extra spaces, no
     trailing characters):

        BaseImage:FROM alpine:3.18
        UserDirective:USER appuser

     Each line must start with the fixed label shown above, immediately followed by
     the exact matching line that now exists in /home/user/app/Dockerfile.

  Summary of deliverables:

    • Modified file:  /home/user/app/Dockerfile
    • New file:       /home/user/policy/update_log.txt   (two precise lines as specified)

  You should be able to accomplish everything with a very small sequence of standard
  shell commands (roughly 2–3 commands is typical).
truth:
Initial state that will exist when the agent starts
  ============================================
  1. Directory /home/user/app already exists.
  2. File /home/user/app/Dockerfile already exists with these exact three lines and a trailing newline:
         FROM alpine:latest
         
         CMD ["echo", "Hello World"]

  3. Directory /home/user/policy does NOT yet exist.

  Target state that automated tests will look for
  ==============================================
  A) /home/user/app/Dockerfile must exist and be world-readable.  Its exact final
     content (including newlines) must be:

         FROM alpine:3.18
         
         CMD ["echo", "Hello World"]
         USER appuser

     Notes:
       • The blank line after the FROM instruction must be preserved.
       • “USER appuser” must be the very last line.

  B) /home/user/policy/update_log.txt must exist and be world-readable.  Its exact
     final two-line content must be:

         BaseImage:FROM alpine:3.18
         UserDirective:USER appuser

     No extra whitespace is allowed before or after each line, and there must be a
     single trailing newline after the second line.

  The automated checker will:
     1. Verify the Dockerfile’s FROM line equals “FROM alpine:3.18”.
     2. Verify the final Dockerfile line equals “USER appuser”.
     3. Verify update_log.txt exists and matches the two-line format above exactly.
